title: be going to (do)
desc: 계획된 의도 또는 근거 있는 예측
examples: We've got our bags packed, so we're going to load them into the trunk and hit the road.
I am at the gas pump, and I'm going to use the novel to fill up the tank.
I'm going to stay in the fast lane for a little while.

title: will / shall
examples: I've got our GPS set, but the first stop will be a gas station, because I want to fill my tank.
No, we'll be all right.
I'll do Coke Zero, please.
I will also do hot black coffee.
I'll go for a medium.

title: will be doing / will have done
examples: I will be giving a tour of the cabin soon, and we will be making lots of videos here.

title: can / could / be able to
examples: We can't leave without getting a road trip snack.
I'm sorry, could you see?
Not everybody loves twizzlers, but I think they're great for road trips, because I can eat them mindlessly.
Can I do a number three meal, please?
So it's easy to hit a deer and you can completely wreck your car if you do.

title: may / might (possibility)
examples: Sometimes on big highways, especially during rush hour, you might encounter some people with road rage.

Usually when somebody gets angry when they're driving, they might lay on the horn or maybe give the middle finger to other drivers, but today is an open road, so I don't have to deal with road rage today.

title: have to / must
examples: I have to select which type of gas I want, and I want regular gas.
The speed limit is a lot higher here, so I have to give it some gas.

Usually when somebody gets angry when they're driving, they might lay on the horn or maybe give the middle finger to other drivers, but today is an open road, so I don't have to deal with road rage today.
You have to pay a toll.

title: should (advice)
examples: OK, should I park over here?

title: would (habit/conditional)
examples: Would you like a bag?

title: Can/Could/Would you...?
examples: I'm sorry, could you see?

title: If I do / If I did
examples: So it's easy to hit a deer and you can completely wreck your car if you do.

title: I wish I knew
examples: I wish they had this on the menu in Korea.

title: Passive (been done / being done)
examples: Nope, I think that will be it.
But fun fact, my husband has been pulled over while driving in the US.

title: have something done
examples: We've got our bags packed, so we're going to load them into the trunk and hit the road.
I've got our GPS set, but the first stop will be a gas station, because I want to fill my tank.

I got my license when I was 16 years old, so I've got a lot of experience driving, but when it comes to going off road on these small dirt paths or trails, I get a little bit nervous because I don't have as much experience.
